input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

### 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime
`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when you swap the merged dictionary:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates on theme change -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes in code like:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

### 2. Activity restart on system theme change
Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when the UI mode changes. Tell it to handle the change itself by adding `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in **`MainActivity.cs`**:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode      // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `UiMode` declared, the activity is **not** recreated — instead you get a callback you can use to detect the system change and swap your dictionary.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors from their ResourceDictionary themes. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and doesn't update when the underlying resource changes. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead, which listens for changes and updates the UI automatically.

2. **App restarting on Android system theme change**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android settings, the app restarts. This is because Android recreates the Activity by default when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. 

To prevent this restart, they need to add `ConfigChanges` to their `MainActivity` to tell Android they'll handle the configuration change themselves rather than letting the system recreate the activity.
